Documentation.Summary: Element at the front of the buffer - this[0].
Documentation.Return: The value of the element of type T at the front of the buffer.

Documentation.Summary: Element at the back of the buffer - this[Size - 1].
Documentation.Return: The value of the element of type T at the back of the buffer.

Documentation.Summary:
Removes the element at the back of the buffer. Decreasing the 
Buffer size by 1.

Removes the element at the front of the buffer. Decreasing the 
Buffer size by 1.
Clears the contents of the array. Size = 0, Capacity is unchanged.

Copies the buffer contents to an array, according to the logical
contents of the buffer (i.e. independent of the internal 
order/contents)
Documentation.Return: A new array with a copy of the buffer contents.

Documentation.Summary:
Get the contents of the buffer as 2 ArraySegments.
Respects the logical contents of the buffer, where
each segment and items in each segment are ordered
according to insertion.
            
Fast: does not copy the array elements.
Useful for methods like <c>Send(IList<ArraySegment<Byte>>)</c>.

<remarks>Segments may be empty.</remarks>
Documentation.Return: An IList with 2 segments corresponding to the buffer content.

Documentation.Summary:
Returns a struct-based enumerator that iterates through this buffer without any heap allocation.
The compiler's duck-typing for <see langword="foreach" /> will prefer this overload over the interface
methods, so <c>foreach (var x in buffer)</c> is zero-alloc.
Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Sandbox.Utility.CircularBuffer`1" /> class.

Maximum capacity of the buffer. Elements pushed into the buffer after
maximum capacity is reached (IsFull = true), will remove an element.

Boolean indicating if Circular is at full capacity.
Adding more elements when the buffer is full will
cause elements to be removed from the other end
of the buffer.
True if has no elements.
Current buffer size (the number of elements that the buffer has).
Circular buffer, push pop and index access is always O(1).

Zero-allocation enumerator for <see cref="T:Sandbox.Utility.CircularBuffer`1" />.
Returned as a value type so <see langword="foreach" /> never allocates.
2D Perlin noise function. For a thread-safe alternative with more options, use .
Documentation.Return: Resulting noise at given coordinates, in range of 0 to 1.

Documentation.Summary: 3D Perlin noise function. For a thread-safe alternative with more options, use .
Documentation.Return: Resulting noise at given coordinates, in range of 0 to 1.

Documentation.Summary: 2D Simplex noise function. For a thread-safe alternative with more options, use .
Documentation.Return: Resulting noise at given coordinates, in range of 0 to 1.

Documentation.Summary: 3D Simplex noise function. For a thread-safe alternative with more options, use .
Documentation.Return: Resulting noise at given coordinates, in range of 0 to 1.

Documentation.Summary: Fractional Brownian Motion noise, a.k.a. Fractal Perlin noise. For a thread-safe alternative with more options, use with .
Documentation.Return: Resulting noise at given coordinates, in range of 0 to 1.

Params.octaves: Number of octaves for the noise. Higher values are slower but produce more detailed results. 3 is a good starting point.

Documentation.Summary: Fractional Brownian Motion noise, a.k.a. Fractal Perlin noise.

Params.octaves: Number of octaves for the noise. Higher values are slower but produce more detailed results. 3 is a good starting point.

Documentation.Summary: Creates a Value noise field, effectively smoothly sampled white noise. Use a for the field to have multiple octaves.
Creates a Perlin noise field, which smoothly samples a grid of random gradients. Use a for the field to have multiple octaves.
Creates a Simplex noise field, a cheaper gradient noise function similar to . Use a for the field to have multiple octaves.

Provides access to coherent noise utilities.
            
All of these functions should return between 0 and 1.

Parameters for constructing a noise field. Use <see cref="T:Sandbox.Utility.Noise.FractalParameters" /> if you
want a noise field made from multiple octaves.

Params.Seed: Seed state to initialize the field with.
Params.Frequency: How quickly should samples change across space.

Documentation.Summary: Seed state to initialize the field with.
How quickly should samples change across space.

Parameters for constructing a noise field. Use <see cref="T:Sandbox.Utility.Noise.FractalParameters" /> if you
want a noise field made from multiple octaves.

Params.Seed: Seed state to initialize the field with.
Params.Frequency: How quickly should samples change across space.

Documentation.Summary: Parameters for constructing a fractal noise field, which layers multiple octaves of a noise function with increasing frequency and reducing amplitudes.

Params.Seed: Seed state to initialize the field with.
Params.Frequency: How quickly should samples change across space.
Params.Octaves: How many layers of noise to use.
Params.Gain: How much to multiply the amplitude of each successive octave by.
Params.Lacunarity: How much to multiply the frequency of each successive octave by.

Documentation.Summary: How many layers of noise to use.
How much to multiply the amplitude of each successive octave by.
How much to multiply the frequency of each successive octave by.
Parameters for constructing a fractal noise field, which layers multiple octaves of a noise function with increasing frequency and reducing amplitudes.

Params.Seed: Seed state to initialize the field with.
Params.Frequency: How quickly should samples change across space.
Params.Octaves: How many layers of noise to use.
Params.Gain: How much to multiply the amplitude of each successive octave by.
Params.Lacunarity: How much to multiply the frequency of each successive octave by.

Documentation.Summary: Sample at a 1D position.
Sample at a 2D position.
Sample at a 3D position.
Sample at a 2D position.
Sample at a 3D position.

A noise function that can be sampled at a 1-, 2-, or 3D position.
Samples will be between <c>0</c> and <c>1</c>. Thread-safe.
Generates a CRC32 checksum from a string.

Params.str: The input to generate a checksum for.

Documentation.Summary: Generates a CRC64 checksum from a byte array.
Generate 64-bit Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC64) checksums.
The number of bytes processed so far.
The total number of bytes to process.
The number of bytes processed since the last progress update.
Progress as a fraction from 0.0 to 1.0.

Provides progress information for operations that process blocks of data,
such as file uploads, downloads, or large data transfers.
